LLM.responsibility: Generating intent (HLX/HLXL), formulating logic, requesting operations.
LLM.restriction: MUST NOT hallucinate handle contents. MUST NOT attempt to parse raw LC streams manually.

Runtime.responsibility: Storing values (CAS), resolving handles, enforcing invariants, validating contracts.
Runtime.authority: Absolute source of truth for data integrity and state.

handles.subscript_rule: ASCII digits 0-9 map to Unicode subscripts ₀-₉.
handles.resolution: Handles are opaque pointers to immutable content-addressed blobs.

tables.definition: A Latent Table is a scoped namespace mapping Handles to canonical HLX-Lite Values.

rules: R1: Determinism. collapse(V) always yields the same internal hash for identical V.
R2: Stability. Handles persist across session turns if the Table context is preserved.
R3: Canonicalization. Object keys MUST be sorted alphabetically/numerically before hashing.
R4: Numeric Normalization. 1.0 and 1 must hash identically if the type system allows (HLX-Lite distinguishes INT/FLOAT, so they differ).

4_ls_operation_semantics.description: Formal definition of Latent Space (LS) operations (LS0-LS20).

LS4.name: Pipeline
LS4.desc: Surface syntax for chaining LS ops.

LS6.name: Batch Resolve
LS6.desc: Atomic resolution of array of handles.

LS8.name: Guard
LS8.desc: Asserts condition on resolved value; halts on fail.

LS9.name: Match
LS9.desc: Structural pattern matching on resolved values.

LS11.name: Validate
LS11.desc: Checks if a handle exists in the table.

LS12.name: Fingerprint
LS12.desc: Returns raw content hash of a handle.

LS13.name: Fork
LS13.desc: Creates a copy of a table.

LS14.name: Merge
LS14.desc: Merges diffs back into parent table.

5_lc_semantics_and_codec.definition: LC (Latent Collapse) is a binary-safe stream format for HLX-Lite values. It encodes VALUES, not logic.
rules: Object fields must be emitted in sorted index order.
Contract IDs and Field Indices are integers.

must_pass.rule: encode(v) is stable across calls and time.
Semantically equal values produce bitwise identical LC.

codes.E_HANDLE_NOT_FOUND: Handle does not exist in the specified table.
codes.E_LC_PARSE: Stream contains invalid markers or structure.
codes.E_CONTRACT_UNKNOWN: Contract ID not recognized by Schema Registry.
codes.E_CANONICALIZATION_FAIL: Input value cannot be canonicalized (e.g., circular ref).

8_test_vectors.description: Mandatory test cases for Runtime conformance.

9_llm_usage_directives.text: The HLX Runtime is the source of truth. Do not hallucinate handle contents. Treat LC streams as opaque binary blobs. Always use 'ls.resolve' or '⚯' to inspect data. If a handle is unknown, catch the E_HANDLE_NOT_FOUND error.
